commands.review_summary:
The PR drops invalid Copilot native Responses reasoning item IDs during replay and adds a focused regression test covering overlong reasoning IDs plus existing tool-call ID normalization.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main replays JSON reasoning signatures with IDs intact while Copilot replay IDs stay enabled, matching the linked issue's structural probe of overlong reasoning IDs. I did not run the test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label and lack of a discrete repair finding make this a maintainer review/merge-gate item, not an automated fix-PR candidate.

target.title: fix(agents): strip invalid Copilot reasoning replay ids
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: GitHub Copilot native Responses replay could keep provider-generated `reasoning.id` values that were longer than Copilot accepts, even after tool-call replay IDs were repaired.
- Why it matters: Copilot rejected the request body before generation with opaque `invalid_request_body` errors, so affected web chat and scheduled agent turns could not continue.
- What changed: Copilot Responses replay now drops invalid reasoning item IDs while preserving valid item replay and existing tool-call ID normalization.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change non-Copilot replay behavior, tool-call ID mapping, provider routing, or session-file repair.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83220
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: GitHub Copilot native Responses replay no longer leaves invalid overlong `reasoning.id` values in the outbound payload after overlong replay tool IDs are normalized.
- Real environment tested: Local Windows source worktree using Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw source branch `bug-019-copilot-replay-reasoning-ids`, model route represented as `github-copilot/gpt-5.5` native Responses.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src\agents\openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "strips invalid Copilot Responses reasoning ids" --reporter=verbose`; then `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src\agents\openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "overlong Copilot Responses replay" --reporter=verbose`; then `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal capture:

```shell
RUN  v4.1.6 C:/OpenClaw/worktrees/bug-019-copilot-replay-reasoning-ids
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts > openai transport stream > strips invalid Copilot Responses reasoning ids after replay tool id repair 158ms
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 332 skipped (334)
Duration  20.47s

RUN  v4.1.6 C:/OpenClaw/worktrees/bug-019-copilot-replay-reasoning-ids
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts > openai transport stream > normalizes overlong Copilot Responses replay tool ids before dispatch 153ms
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts > openai transport stream > keeps distinct overlong Copilot Responses replay item ids distinct 3ms
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 330 skipped (334)
Duration  20.27s

git diff --check
# no output; exit 0
```

- Observed result after fix: The regression fixture builds a Copilot Responses payload from replay history containing both an invalid overlong reasoning ID and overlong tool IDs; the reasoning item has no outbound `id`, every remaining outbound `id` and `call_id` is alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen and length <= 64, and the normalized `function_call_output.call_id` still matches the normalized `function_call.call_id`.
- What was not tested: A live Copilot replay against the private affected agent session was not rerun because it requires private session history and provider credentials. Broad `pnpm check:changed` was not run locally because this is a Codex worktree; the scoped Vitest proof and diff hygiene cover the touched transport seam.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Redacted runtime evidence from 2026-05-17 showed Copilot returning `400 {"message":"","code":"invalid_request_body"}` with `providerRuntimeFailureKind="schema"`. A sanitized structural probe of the affected session showed outbound payload counts after the previous tool-ID fix: `functionCalls=114`, `functionOutputs=114`, `callIdsOver64=0`, `orphanOutputs=0`, `duplicateIds=0`, but `idsOver64=53` with all overlong IDs on `reasoning` items and `maxIdLen=408`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `convertResponsesMessages()` replayed same-model Responses reasoning items by parsing `thinkingSignature` and pushing the item unchanged. The prior Copilot replay fix normalized tool-call IDs but did not sanitize provider-visible `reasoning.id` values.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Copilot replay tests covered overlong function-call IDs but not persisted overlong reasoning item IDs.
- Contributing context (if known): Copilot reports this invalid request shape as an opaque `invalid_request_body`, so once tool IDs were repaired the remaining reasoning ID failures no longer named the exact offending field.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Copilot Responses replay history containing an invalid overlong `reasoning.id` plus overlong replay tool IDs produces an outbound payload with no invalid provider-visible item IDs or call IDs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The failure occurs while building the provider request payload before any model generation, so the transport parameter builder is the narrowest deterministic seam.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing overlong Copilot replay tool-ID tests covered the tool half, not reasoning item IDs.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Copilot-backed agent sessions with invalid persisted reasoning item IDs should resume instead of failing before generation with a schema/tool-payload rejection.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[persisted reasoning item id >64 chars] -> [Responses replay payload] -> [Copilot invalid_request_body]

After:
[persisted reasoning item id >64 chars] -> [drop invalid reasoning id during Copilot replay] -> [provider-valid replay payload]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows source worktree for tests; original failing runtime OS/version redacted/not enough info.
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0 local source checkout.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-5.5` native Responses.
- Integration/channel (if any): Agent runtime replay; no external channel surface changed.
- Relevant config (redacted): Provider route `github-copilot` + `openai-responses`; private agent/session identifiers omitted.

### Steps

1. Build a Copilot Responses request from replay history containing an overlong provider-generated reasoning item ID and overlong tool-call IDs.
2. Verify the generated input payload.
3. Run the focused Vitest filters listed above.

### Expected

- No outbound Copilot replay `id` or `call_id` exceeds 64 characters or uses characters outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`; function-call outputs remain paired with normalized function-call IDs.

### Actual

- Before this patch, the payload still contained 53 overlong `reasoning.id` values in the affected session structure after tool-call IDs had been normalized.
- After this patch, the regression fixture removes the invalid reasoning ID and keeps tool-call pairing valid.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Focused Copilot reasoning-ID regression; existing overlong Copilot replay tool-ID tests; `git diff --check`.
- Edge cases checked: Existing distinct overlong tool item IDs still remain distinct; function-call-output `call_id` still matches the normalized function-call `call_id`.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Copilot replay against the private affected session and broad changed gates.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Dropping a reasoning item ID could affect Copilot reasoning replay continuity if Copilot expected that ID.
  - Mitigation: The ID is dropped only when it is invalid for Copilot's request-body ID envelope; keeping it already causes request rejection before generation.
target.author: galiniliev
